hypothesis: Augmenting the ∆-learning XGBoost model with metal-center electronic features computed from GFN1‑xTB — specifically Mulliken spin population, the d‑orbital projected density-of-states centroid (d‑band center), and local magnetic moment — will reduce the HSE06 band gap MAE for Cr/Mn/Fe/Co/Ni-containing MOFs by at least 30% relative to the ∆-learning model trained only on ST‑135, RACs and APRDF descriptors.
